source: NvChad - Overview: what NvChad is, its philosophy, Lua, updating, theme switching

options: A ready-made configuration (packaged as a Neovim plugin) that turns Neovim into an IDE
A fork of the Vim editor
questions.why: NvChad is a base configuration for Neovim, delivered inside the lazy.nvim plugin manager.

questions.q: How do the NvChad developers describe the project's purpose?
options: Only a base on which to build your own personal IDE
A drop-in replacement for Neovim itself
A theme pack and nothing more
questions.why: NvChad is meant as a starting base; further customization is done with plugins.

questions.q: What language is NvChad's configuration written in?
questions.why: Lua is fast and integrates seamlessly into Neovim's configuration.

questions.q: Why are NvChad's startup and command times so fast?
options: Lazy loading loads plugins only when their functionality is required
It is compiled to a native binary
It disables all plugins by default
It runs on a remote server
questions.why: The Lazy loading technique defers plugins until needed.
NvChad ships a git-based update mechanism invoked with :NvChadUpdate.

questions.q: Which key combination is used to change the theme?
questions.why: Themes are selected with the <leader> + th mapping.

questions.q: When NvChad talks about 'customization', it refers to...
options: functionality, not the appearance of the interface
questions.why: Customization means adapting functionality; the UI look is a separate matter.

questions.q: NvChad's automatic update mechanism is built on top of...
questions.why: Updates (and the config sync model) use git.
